head: e9fc50e
scope: merge-resolution review: origin/main (a11y #1596, release #1601, tab-revival #1532) merged into pin-tab branch; conflicts in README.md, commands.md, browser.rs (tab_switch/tab_close)
verification: tab_close signature change (main added dialog_session) — all call sites in diffed files; fixed our stale test caller tab_close(None,None)
tab_close successor-revival guarded by !bound_target_is_gone(): non-pin/pin-alive sessions still revive (guard false); pin+gone skips touching the neighbor — reasoned + e2e
tab_switch bind_active_target() rebind verified live: pinned session switching by targetId re-binds, binding file updated, pinned=true
e2e both features green: e2e_pin_tab_gone/rebinds/auto_attached_foreign + tab_new_with_label switch/close + switch_rejects_bare_integer

exemptions.evidence: AGENTS.md allows emdash sparingly, forbids only '--'; all flagged lines use ' — ' in prose/error strings with base precedent

issue_candidates.title: shared-Chrome tab list returns all tabs incl. other sessions'; tabs[0] is not the session's own bound tab
issue_candidates.evidence: dogfood: A's tab list[0] was the host tab; naive tabs[0] operates on a foreign tab
issue_candidates.scope: pre-existing shared-Chrome semantics, not introduced by this PR

issue_candidates.title: non-pin session connect+open navigates the existing active tab (can clobber host/other session)
issue_candidates.evidence: dogfood step: C without --pin-tab navigated the host tab
issue_candidates.scope: by-design; pin is the isolation opt-in
